sourceMovie.title: Love Exposure
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Love Exposure for the same reason it broke through in 2008: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a comedy rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Sion Sono keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The action and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Arthur
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Love Exposure clicked for you, Arthur (1981) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and drama, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Love Exposure?
faq.answer: Try Arthur, Mind Game, Bottle Rocket—each shares DNA with Love Exposure while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Love Exposure?
faq.answer: Love Exposure (2008) is best described as Action, Comedy, Drama, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Love Exposure worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 8.0/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
